hypothesis: If an LLM-based agent framework equipped with domain-specific external tools (periodic table, knowledge base, and memory modules) iteratively guides compositional proposals and receives property feedback, then it will achieve higher success rates in targeting specific formation energies and generate more valid, unique, and novel inorganic materials compared to tool-free LLM variants and non-iterative diffusion-based generative models, because the structured retrieval of chemical knowledge combined with iterative self-refinement enables more chemically informed and directed exploration of the compositional space.
